title: Quenching autofluorescence with Cupric Sulfate
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"a1hnr","text":"This protocol describes how to quench lipofuscin autofluorescence from brain sections. The protocol works for mouse and human brain tissue. We find this method, unlike other methods for quenching autofluorescence, does not diminish desired fluorescence from secondary antibodies. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2edql","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"oxaqx","text":"Reagents required to prepare Cupric sulfate solution: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":54}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"atpj8","text":"10 mM CuSO4","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ejvc0","text":"50 mM ammonium acetate, pH, 5.0","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c3etd","text":"Store indefinitely at 4°C","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Perform after secondary antibody incubation and 3 times 00:05:00 rinses with TBS Contributed by users . ( 00:05:00 , 00:05:00 )

Place Cupric sulfate solution Contributed by users into wells of a six well tissue culture plate. Transfer the tissue sections into the wells and incubate at 4 °C with shaking for 00:30:00 . This step reduces autofluorescence from lipofuscin granules in the tissue (Schnell et al., 1999).

Transfer the tissue to the wells of a tissue culture plate containing TBS Contributed by users . Rinse the tissue three to four times in TBS with shaking for about 00:10:00 . ( 00:10:00 , 00:10:00 , 00:10:00 ).
Mount tissue on slides and coverslip with mounting media Contributed by users .
creator: Laura Volpicelli-Daley
